key_insight: Tensorion generalizes Muon from matrices to higher-order tensors by using a tensor-norm-constrained linear minimization oracle that preserves the tensor structure during optimization.
engineering_relevance: This can improve training stability and convergence for models with naturally tensor-shaped weights, especially in computer vision and other structured learning settings.
method: Formulates each update as steepest descent over a tensor norm ball.
Chooses a norm that tightly controls the tensor spectral norm while keeping the LMO tractable.
Computes the LMO via adaptively selected unfolding matrices; reduces exactly to Muon for matrices.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on tensor-based computer vision problems, where Tensorion showed improved convergence behavior and more stable gradient updates than Adam-based and existing tensor-aware baselines.
watch_item: Practical performance may depend on the cost and stability of selecting unfoldings and solving the tensor LMO efficiently at scale.
paper_title: Tensorion: A Tensor-Aware Generalization of the Muon Optimizer
